title: TaskCancelled payload
description: Emitted when a task is cancelled via task_cancel without completing. Distinct from TaskCompleted — completed means the work was done; cancelled means the task is being removed from consideration (out-of-scope, duplicate, infeasible). The envelope's rationale answers 'why cancel' for audit; the payload's reason is the user-visible category.

chain_slug.description: Chain slug disambiguating which chain's task this event targets, so the fold scopes to one task rather than fanning out across same-slug tasks in the project (bug task-lifecycle-event-folds-fan-out-across-duplicate-task-slugs). Optional: pre-fix events omit it and the fold falls back to the legacy slug+project match.

reason.description: Why the task was cancelled. Free text — no closed enum (the cancellation cases are diverse: 'subsumed by sibling task', 'out of scope after design pivot', 'feature shipped through different path', etc.). Optional in this schema because the current task_cancel handler does not require a reason from callers; future T3 rationale tightening will gate on envelope.rationale rather than this payload field.
